sections.paragraph:
for עְאֹון; as Psa 144:13 בתיב; comm. (see note) a collect. noun, flocks, small cattle, i.e. sheep and goats (So Arab. ضَأْنُ, ضَأَنُ is wool-producing cattle, opp. to goats; but ااضان اجبايلى denotes, however, the wild goat. For sheep and goats = צאֹן they commonly use غَنَمُ; Syr. ܥܳܢܐܳ id.), Gen 4:2 26:14 29:2 and very frequently. Opp. to בָּקָר herds, which see, Gen 27:9 Levit. 1:10 22:21 (compare 19 Lev 22:19); rarely used only of sheep, 1Sa 25:2. To this collective (which is also used with numerals) corresponds the noun of unity שֶׂה a sheep, or goat. Exod. 21:37 , “if any one steal a sheep (שֶׂה), he shall restore four sheep” (אַרְבַּע צאֹן); Eze 45:15.

Note. As to gender, it is joined with a masc., where it means rams and he-goats. Gen 30:39, וַיֶּתֱמוּ הַצּאֹן “and the rams (and he-goats) rutted;” with a fem., where it means ewes and she-goats; ibid., וַתֵּלַרְןָ הַצּאֹן “and the sheep brought forth;” but, Gen 31:10, it is joined, like epicœne nouns, with a masc., although it means ewes.
